LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS COMPLAINANTS ALJOY, INC. & PETER GLIKSHTERN'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER PRECLUDING PLAINTIFF FROM INTRODUCING EXPERT TESTIMONY AT TRIAL, OR ALTERNATIVELY, COMPELLING DISCLOSURE OF EXPERT WITNESS INFORMATION IS GRANTED, IN PART. IF ANY PARTY INTENDS TO RELY ON EXPERT TESTIMONY AT TRIAL, THAT PARTY SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAME(S) OF THE EXPERT(S) AND A SUMMARY OF THE ANTICIPATED TESTIMONY NO LATER THAN JANUARY 15, 2014 AT 5 PM. ABSENT THE PARTIES AGREEING TO A DIFFERENT SCHEDULE, ANY EXPERT DISCLOSED BY PLAINTIFF WILL BE MADE AVAILABLE FOR DEPOSITION NO LATER THAN JANUARY 16, 2014 AT 5 PM, AND ANY EXPERT DISCLOSED BY DEFENDANTS WILL BE MADE AVAILABLE FOR DEPOSITION NO LATER THAN JANUARY 17, 2014 AT 5 PM. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WERE SOUGHT BY MOVING PARTY AND NONE WILL BE IMPOSED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

ORDER GRANTING IN PART AND DENYING IN PART PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO COMPEL VERIFIED RESPONSES AND FOR SANCTIONS

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS FRESCO MUSIC, LLC, JACOBO JUAREZ, NHU TRUONG'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT'S VERIFIED RESPONSES TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY IS GRANTED IN PART. C.C.P. 2030.220 REQUIRES AN INDIVIDUAL TO PROVIDE VERIFICATIONS BASED ON HIS/HER PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE. MR. GLIKSHTERN SHALL PROVIDE THE PROPER VERIFICATIONS TO THE DISCOVERY AT ISSUE. THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS DENIED AS TO DEFENDANT ALJOY, INC. AS THE ISSUE IS MOOT. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED AS THE COURT FINDS SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION IN PROVIDING THE VERIFICATIONS LATE. ORDER SIGNED. PRO-TEM: ARLENE BARTON; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Moiton For A Protective Order Precluding Pltf From Introducing Expert Testimony At Trial, Or Alternatively, Compelling Disclosure Of Expert Witness Information
Off Calendar Jan-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: HARRY LEWIS, ESQ., 415.625.5126, FOR PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANTS FRESCO MUSIC, LLC AND JACOBO JUAREZ AND PLAINTIFF NHU TRUONG.
JAMES NEUDECKER, ESQ., 415.659.4817, FOR DEFENDANTS/CROSS COMPLAINANTS ALJOY, INC. AND PETER GLIKSHTERN.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS COMPLAINANTS ALJOY, INC. & PETER GLIKSHTERN'S MOTION For A Protective Order Precluding PLAINTIFF From Introducing Expert Testimony At Trial, Or Alternatively, Compelling Disclosure Of Expert Witness Information IS
Granted, in part. If any party intends to rely on expert testimony at trial, that party shall disclose the name(s) of the expert(s) and a summary of the anticipated testimony no later than January 15, 2014 at 5 pm. Absent the parties agreeing to a different schedule, any expert disclosed by Plaintiff will be made available for deposition no later than January 16, 2014 at 5 pm, and any expert disclosed by Defendants will be made available for deposition no later than January 17, 2014 at 5 pm. No monetary sanctions were sought by moving party and none will be imposed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Mtn To Compel Deft'S Verified Responses To Written Disco
Off Calendar Sep-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James M. Neudecker, ReedSmith, 415.659.4817, appearing for Defendant, Aljoy, Inc.
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

The Court adopts the Tentative Ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFS FRESCO MUSIC, LLC, JACOBO JUAREZ, NHU TRUONG'S Motion To Compel Defendant's Verified Responses To Written Discovery is Granted in part. C.C.P. 2030.220 requires an individual to provide verifications based on his/her personal knowledge. Mr. Glikshtern shall provide the proper verifications to the discovery at issue. The motion to compel is denied as to defendant Aljoy, Inc. as the issue is moot. The request for sanctions is denied as the Court finds substantial justification in providing the verifications late. Order signed. Pro-Tem: Arlene Barton; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion Andm Motion To Compel Defts Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set No One And Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. HARRY G. LEWIS OF THE CORNERSTONE LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 625-5126 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. JAMES M. NEUDECKER OF REED SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 659-4817 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court adopted its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: Plaintiffs FRESCO MUSIC, LLC'S, JACOBO JUAREZ'S, and NHU TRUONG'S Motion to Compel Defendants Further Responses to Request for Production of Documents, Set No. One and Motion for Sanctions is Granted in part.
Aljoy shall electronically produce all documents that it has already produced or is producing in response to Fresco's first set of document requests. Aljoy's electronic production shall comply with plaintiff's protocol except that native files need not be Bates stamped.
Aljoy shall notify the court at the hearing whether it wants placed under a protective order any of the additional documents that it is being ordered to produce.
All documents and responses that this order orders Aljoy to provide shall be provided by September 18.
Requests 1-5, 29-31, and 38-39. Denied.
Requests 10-11 IS GRANTED.
Requests 12-17. Denied.
Requests 18-19. Granted in part. Aljoy shall amend its responses to state that it is producing and shall produce all documents showing funds Aljoy transferred from SOM to Metropol or that Aljoy used to pay Metropol's debts.
Request 24: Granted in part. Aljoy shall amend its response to state that it is producing and shall produce all documents related to any California Department of Alcoholic Beverage Control violations or investigations prior to the 2010 citation referred to in Request 24. Aljoy shall amend its response to state that it is producing these documents.
Request 25 and 27: Denied without prejudice to more narrowly written requests.
Requests 32, 34, and 36: Granted in part. Aljoy shall amend its responses to state that it is producing and shall produce all nonprivileged responsive documents that were written by or to Aljoy or that relate to SOM.
Requests 33, 35, and 37: Granted in part. Aljoy shall amend its responses to state that it is producing and shall produce all responsive documents that were written by or to Aljoy or that relate to SOM.
Request 40: Granted. Aljoy will produce the report that it agreed to produce in its response to Request 40. Aljoy shall also amend its response to state that it is producing and shall produce the ESI version of the August 23, 2010 letter in the manner that this Order requires it to produce ESI as indicated above. Sanctions are denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro-tem : Bruce Highman; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/JPT
=(302/JPT)
